title: Lovable vs MeDo, which AI app builder actually works?

segments.text: AI app builders are everywhere right now
and everyone's claiming that you can go
from an idea to a full product in just
minutes. Well, today I want to actually
test that. So, in this video I'm going
to be comparing Midoo to Lovable
head-to-head to see which one really
delivers when you try to build real
applications, not just simple demos.
Now, I'm going to be running the same
prompts through both of these tools and
judging them based on speed, how
complete the output is, how close the
result is to something you could
actually push into production, and how
much it costs to run through these
tools. I'm going to be trying to give as
fair of a comparison as I possibly can.
And I'm going to use a simple set of
metrics across all of the tests that I
do. So, first we're going to look at
completeness. So, are we just getting a
front end or are we getting a front end,
backend, database, things that actually
connect together when we try to build,
for example, a full stack app? Second,
integration support. So, are these
working with plugins, APIs, external
services? Does it work with Stripe, for
example? That's one of the major tests
we're going to look at. Then, there's a
big one. How close is the result to a
production ready app? So, how many bugs
are there? Do I need to go back and
forth with a lot of prompts, etc. And
then, finally, I want to look at the
cost. So, not just the pure subscription
price, but how many credits it actually
takes to build something and what that
translates to in dollars. Because I
found that after working with a lot of
these tools, often times you'll get a
similar result from the LLM because a
lot of times they're using the same
models in the back end. However, the
cost can be very different depending on
how they've set up the credits, their
billing, their subscriptions, etc. So,
with that said, let's dive into it and
let me introduce the two tools that
we'll be comparing. So, the first tool
that we'll have a look at here is
Lovable. Now, I'm sure a lot of you are
familiar with this, but this is a noode
AI app builder where you can go in here,
you can type something like hello, you
know, build me this, whatever, and it
goes ahead and builds it. It has the
ability to build full stack applications
as well. It can connect to databases, it
can connect to GitHub, very popular, but
also quite expensive as you're going to
see in this video. And then the second
tool that we're going to be having a
look at is a newer tool that was
recently released called Mido. Now, full
disclosure, Mido did reach out to me to
make this video. However, they didn't
tell me anything I need to say in the
video. They purely said, "Just compare
us to some other tools out there and
tell us what you think. Be 100% honest."
That's exactly what I'm going to do in
this video. Now, Mido is very similar to
Lovable. However, it is a little bit
cheaper, as you're going to see as we go
through some of the examples. It's meant
to be more beginner friendly, have more
built-in integrations, and be capable of
building larger full stack applications,
whereas Lovable focuses a lot on the
design and the aesthetics. Midu focuses
more on the logic and actually building
something that is more complete and that
has all of those connections, the
databases, the backend, all of that kind
of stuff. So, let's see if that's true
in this video. Now, if you want to sign
up for either of these platforms, I will
leave links in the description. Both of
them have free credits, and that's
actually the first thing that I want to
start with is how you can use these for
free. So, you can play around with them
and see if you like them. So, with me,
when you sign up with a free account,
you don't need a credit card or
anything. you get 300 credits for free.
Now, in order to generate an application
and run a query to Medu that costs 15
credits. So, you can actually create
quite a few things in this free plan.
And anytime you sign in, so every single
day you sign in, you get additional free
credits. So, if we just look at the
credit earning rules here, you can see
you get 300 credits when you create a
new user. If you log in, you get 100
credits. So, that's every single day you
can get up to 100 additional credits to
use. For every user you invite, you can
get 300 credits up to 3,000 per day. And
then it shows you the um cost for doing
some of these different operations,
right? So, it's very transparent and you
know exactly how much it's going to
cost. Now, with Lovable, if you create a
free account, you get five free credits
to start and then I believe every day
you sign in, you get an additional five
free credits. So, that's kind of how it
works. Now, the credits do not directly
compare because lovable credits are more
valuable than the meu credits because
you use less of them when you're
generating an app. However, I'm going to
go through a cost analysis at the end of
the video so you can see how much
credits actually cost you if you were
paying for them and how much it costs to
generate an application on both of these
platforms. Okay, so with that in mind,
what I'm going to do is I'm going to
start running the first prompt through
these tools. The first thing I want to
do is just try to generate a really
simple landing page. So just purely
front end and see how these platforms
handle that. All right. So on my screen
I have a super simple prompt. Build a
clean modern front end for a SAS landing
page called focus flow. I go through a
few requirements. Nothing too complex. I
just want to see what these platforms do
with something basic like this. So I'm
going to go ahead and copy this into
both MU as well as lovable. I'll quickly
show you the process. Then let's look at
the finished result. So let's start with
MU here. What I'll do is I'll just paste
this in. When I do that, it's going to
think for a second and it's going to
start generating a readme file with a
plan for what it's going to create. So,
you can see that it's created this
requirements file that's a little bit
more in-depth from what I gave it. Now,
at this point, what I can do is edit
this if I want to, right? So, I can just

ahead and press on generate app. So, I'm
going to go ahead and press on continue
and then it's going to start generating
the front end for me. Now, let's go over
to Lovable and let's do the same thing.
So, from lovable I can paste in the
prompt. I can also attach some kind of
theme. So that's something a little bit

to go ahead and press enter and let's

so I can see exactly what it looks like
kind of full screen in my browser. So
let's actually have a look at it here.
All right, so we have the Focus Flow

This was just a basic prompt. I just

So, this is the version that we get with

sorry accomplished the goal. If I press

really didn't struggle with them at all.
They took a very similar amount of time
to complete this. And if you've worked

can publish the site directly. You can
ask for additional things just like me
do. You can do the visual edit here if
you want. You can add in images, right?
Take screenshots, chat with the model,
etc. Now, in terms of credits used, this

that's approximately half of the credits
that we had. Whereas with Mido, again,
that used 15 credits to generate the

lovable to achieve a very similar result
as it is for media. Now, anyways, this

let's see the result that we get. Okay,
so both of these tools have finished

and I was just walking through them and
you can see in this case with me, I
ended up giving this nine more prompts
in order to get it to fully finish and

about seven, eight more prompts to fix

to work in MEU. So, I'm not giving up on

process that when I tried to generate

Cool. So let's pop this out into preview

From here, I can manage users. So, I can

In this admin panel here, I'm able to

Again, it worked with the Stripe

Stripe key that is right here. Cool. So,
that's MU. Now, let's move over here and

Okay. Okay, so Lovable, very similar to

For example, if I press this upgrade to
